utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good alright hello hannah how are you today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're doing well

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did have this this hacking cough for about three days now

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay tell me more about that cough

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it started three days ago i was around my sister and she has been sick with the same kind of symptoms and so this cough has just started off with like a gradual cough and now i've just been coughing up some green phlegm here

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay so you you started having some mucus with it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and how about any other symptoms with your cough

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm having a little bit of a sore throat here and then a little bit of a some nasal congestion as well

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay and how about any fevers

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have n't had a fever yet

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no fever okay and then any shortness of breath

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no shortness of breath

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no shortness of breath okay and what have you been doing for your cough

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've just been kinda drinking lots of water taking some cough medication over the counter and it seems like it's not really working

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's not helping okay and tell me do you have any history of any seasonal allergies

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right around spring time i get a little bit of sneezing and the runny runny nose

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay and then any how about any body aches for you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no body aches

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: nausea or vomiting

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright so we will definitely examine you and talk more about that and as far as your diabetes how has everything been going with that checking your sugars how has that been going

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been pretty good at checking my blood sugars they've been running around one twenty range

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay and i know the metformin is a we started you on a lower dose it's the five hundred milligrams it's a new medication for you you've been tolerating it okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i have n't had any problems

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay good any diarrhea sometimes patients will say that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no diarrhea

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no diarrhea okay and then how about as far as your blood pressure

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: blood pressure has been a little bit high i've noticed

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: kind of one forties the top number

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and the lower numbers seems like it's been okay around seventies

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: seventy okay so a bit high and the hydrochlorothiazide have you been able to take that everyday

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i've been taking it everyday

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and how about your diet how's been thing as far as salty foods

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been trying to stay away from the salty foods but sugar intake i'm having a little bit of trouble with that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay but it seems like you've been making good progress and that could be difficult sometimes and you mentioned being around i think you said your sister a few days ago or a family member

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah she was just kinda sick with similar symptoms and i noticed i started to developed a little bit of a cough right after

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: after that okay and how is your family doing how is everyone doing other than that other than being sick for your sister how is everyone going

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: everyone's doing great

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: working busy with life yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and for you too were you on vacation last week and a lot of people had vacation last week

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i was on vacation last week actually

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay hopefully you were n't sick for too much of it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i was feeling okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: got back and started feeling poorly

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sorry about that alright so what i'm gon na do now is i'm gon na go ahead and start your physical exam i looking at your vitals vitals look overall pretty good i do see your blood pressure is elevated at here it's one forty four over seventy two otherwise everything else looks good so let's check you out so first starting i'm just gon na check start with your sinuses and when i press here do you have any pain any tenderness when i do that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no okay so no frontal sinus tenderness how about when i press on the on your cheeks here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: maybe a little bit just a little bit

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on both sides

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so bilateral maxillary sinus tenderness alright and i'm just gon na take a look at in your mouth if you can open up okay so i do see some do have some erythema of the pharynx and the tonsils are symmetrical i do n't appreciate any exudates now i'm just checking your neck and i do appreciate some anterior cervical adenopathy alright and i'm just gon na take a listen to you alright so listening to your heart so you have a nice regular rate and rhythm and you do still have that two out of six systolic ejection murmur at the left base now i'm taking listen to your lungs and on your lungs exam okay your lungs are nice and clear i do n't appreciate any wheezes rales or rhonchi alright and just looking at your lower extremities i do n't appreciate any edema there so let's talk about my assessment and plan so for your first problem of the cough so looking at your cough it does seem like you have a upper respiratory infection and so basically that's a virus most likely it's a virus that's causing your symptoms especially being around someone else who has similar symptoms and seems like most likely they spread it to you so i want you to continue with doing a lot of the time is just letting the virus run it's course and taking things just to help with your symptoms so drinking lots of fluids that's gon na help you trying i know you've been doing a cough medicine you can even try another one you have to be careful with the blood pressure and diabetes so you can try like a coricidin hbp can help with the cough and lots of rest okay lots of rest now we are in a current pandemic and so something i can check for you if you're okay with that is checking for covid if that's okay with you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i'm okay with that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so we will check you for that today and see how you're doing with that and then we will give you those results okay any questions about that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no questions

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no okay so for problem number two the type two diabetes so we'll continue you on your metformin five hundred milligrams daily or twice a day you had blood work at your last visit so you'll be due at your next visit for your hemoglobin a1c and that's the check the sugars on your red blood cells so we do that about every three months so at your next visit we'll check that for you and then we will go from there but it seems like you're doing well with that now as far as problem number three your high blood pressure so your blood pressure is elevated here and it sounds like it's been you know creeping up just a little bit at home as well you're on a very tiny dose of the hydrochlorothiazide so what i would like to do is increase it to twenty five milligrams once a day again i would recommend that you take it in the morning just because you may have noticed it can make you urinate quite a bit and then i would love for you to just continue checking as you have been a few times a week just checking your blood pressure at different times of the day so we can continue to see how you do with that okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay alright that sounds

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright any questions for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no questions

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: thank you alright so at this point now i'm just gon na press the

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na

detail_list.summary: The patient presents with a hacking cough for three days.
Cough
The patient reports a productive cough with green phlegm and exposure to a sick sister.
The doctor asks about other symptoms associated with the cough.
The patient reports sore throat and nasal congestion.
The doctor asks if the patient has a fever.
The patient denies having a fever.
The doctor asks if the patient has shortness of breath.
The patient denies shortness of breath.
The doctor asks what the patient has been doing for the cough.
The patient has been drinking water and taking over-the-counter cough medication without improvement.
The doctor asks about a history of seasonal allergies.
The patient reports seasonal allergies with sneezing and runny nose in the spring.
The doctor asks about body aches.
The patient denies body aches.
The doctor asks about nausea or vomiting.
The patient denies nausea or vomiting.
Diabetes
The doctor asks how the patient is managing her diabetes and blood sugar checks.
The patient reports checking blood sugars, which are around 120.
The patient is taking metformin 500 mg.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced diarrhea.
The patient denies diarrhea.
Hypertension
The patient reports her blood pressure has been high.
The patient reports blood pressure readings in the 140s.
The patient reports diastolic blood pressure readings around 70.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking hydrochlorothiazide every day.
The patient confirms taking hydrochlorothiazide every day.
The doctor asks about the patient's diet and salt intake.
The patient is trying to avoid salty foods but struggles with sugar intake.
The doctor notes elevated blood pressure of 144/72 and begins the physical exam.
The doctor asks if the patient has sinus tenderness.
The patient denies frontal sinus tenderness.
The patient reports mild tenderness when the doctor presses on her cheeks.

Physical exam findings: bilateral maxillary sinus tenderness, erythema of the pharynx, symmetrical tonsils without exudates, anterior cervical adenopathy, regular heart rate and rhythm with 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, and no edema.
The doctor assesses the patient with an upper respiratory infection and advises rest, fluids, and Coricidin HBP.
The doctor asks if the patient consents to a COVID-19 test.
The patient consents to the COVID-19 test.
The doctor continues metformin 500 mg twice a day and increases hydrochlorothiazide to 25 mg once a day.
The patient is due for an A1c test at the next visit and should continue checking blood pressure at home.
